checks.a_analysis: The output document contains text that is present in the source document, such as the title 'Building Footprint Update Status' and the contents of the legend. The metadata title and image alt text are AI-generated, which is explicitly allowed by the audit rules. There is no evidence of fabricated body text or sentences.
The source document does not contain any contact information (phone numbers, emails, addresses, URLs), and none is found in the output. There are no fabrications.

The output document correctly represents the single-page source document. It includes the primary heading, the legend (as a table), and the main graphic (as an image). No entire section or page is missing.

The legend in the source document contains nine distinct text items. The output represents this as a table with one header row and five data rows. All nine text items from the source legend are present within the table cells. The row count is appropriate for the content presented.

checks.a_analysis: The output fails to capture several minor text elements present in the source. Specifically, the department header 'Emergency Management NC DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY' at the top left is omitted from the text elements. Additionally, the specific chart title 'Risk Building Footprint Refresh Current Status 6/16/2025' and the footer status date 'Status as of: 6/16/2025' are missing. While the chart title is partially summarized in the image alt text, the specific date and the organizational headers are not available as text, which constitutes a minor informational loss.

checks.b_evidence_location: Page 1, Header, Map Title, and Footer

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed both the source PDF and the converted JSON output. There are no instances of duplicated text or content blocks in the output. Each structural element (heading, table, and image) appears exactly once, matching its occurrence in the source document.

The output contains a single heading 'Building Footprint Update Status' which is correctly assigned as level 1 (H1). This matches the main title of the source document. No heading levels are skipped or incorrectly nested.

The legend in the source PDF is presented as a multi-column list/table. The output successfully captures this as a table structure. The header 'Legend' is correctly identified. The rows correctly pair the phase information with the status information. Row 5, which contains only one item in the source, is correctly represented as a single cell in the final row. The merging of the color icons with the adjacent text into a single cell is a standard and acceptable structural simplification for accessibility.

The reading order in the JSON output flows logically from the main title to the legend and then to the map image. While the legend is visually positioned in the lower-left corner of the map in the PDF, placing it before the image in the reading sequence is an acceptable optimization for accessibility, ensuring the user understands the symbols before viewing the graphic.
The source document does not contain any traditional bulleted or numbered lists. The list-like content in the legend has been correctly converted into a table structure.
The source document does not contain any hyperlinks, and no links have been injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully reproduces the visual organization of the source PDF. The primary components—the document title, the map graphic, and the legend—are all present and logically ordered. While the legend from the map has been moved into a separate table for accessibility, and the map itself is rendered as an image, the visual flow and relationship between the information remain consistent with the source. The inclusion of logos as separate images at the bottom rather than their original positions is a standard result of responsive HTML layout and does not impede information retrieval.

The legend, which appears as a graphic box within the map in the source PDF, has been correctly extracted and rendered as a structured HTML table. The table is visually intact with clear row and column alignment, making the information easily readable. There are no overlapping cells or misaligned headers.

All meaningful images from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the main North Carolina map graphic, the NC Department of Public Safety (DPS) logo, the EMAP Accredited seal, and the North Carolina Emergency Management triangle logo. No images are broken or missing.

There is no visual duplication of body content. While the document title appears at the very top (as a navigation/page title) and again as the main heading, this is expected accessible design. The core content (the table and the map) appears only once.
